# Runbook — RFQ generation path (`rfq_poll`) (a composed RFQ stuck / refused / dark) (Successor-Remediation, Op Stds §43)

<!-- BEGIN TREE-XREF (generated by scripts/build_runbook_xrefs.py — do not edit inside) -->
> **In the troubleshooting tree** ([printable guide](../troubleshooting/troubleshooting_guide.md) · dashboard `/troubleshoot`). This runbook resolves:
>   - Purchase order — build, config, pull/render/file, send (dark) → rfq-poll renders and files a composed request-for-quote per vendor (dark) → “Composed RFQs are not being pulled/filed.”
>   - Purchase order — build, config, pull/render/file, send (dark) → rfq-poll renders and files a composed request-for-quote per vendor (dark) → “An RFQ (or one vendor's copy) is refused / missing.”
> Daemon(s): `rfq-poll` — see the [daemon reference](../references/daemon_reference.md).
<!-- END TREE-XREF -->

A §43 successor-remediation entry, written for the **Successor-Operator**: a trained
operator who runs Claude Code and reads Smartsheet rows + alert emails, but does **not**
read code. Claude loads the relevant block to drive a Tier-2 repair; the operator sees the
ITS_Errors / ITS_Review_Queue / ITS_Daemon_Health / RFQ_Log evidence and approves.
The §42 code-reader rationale lives in the module docstring of
`po_materials/rfq_poll.py`.

`po_materials/rfq_poll.py` is part of the **generation half of the External Send Gate**
(FM v11 Invariant 1) — it is AI-free (cloud **and** local) and customer-send-free. The
office composes an RFQ (request for quote) in the portal; the Cloudflare Worker
(`safety_portal/worker/rfq.ts`) queues it **send-free** in D1 and signs it (`rfq:v1`);
this launchd daemon (`org.solutionsmith.its.rfq-poll`, every 120 s) is the Mac-side
consumer, behind ONE gate (`po_materials.rfq_poll.polling_enabled`, shipped `false`).
Per RFQ it:

1. **Verifies** the row (rfq:v1 HMAC, rebuilt from the served fields) — a failure is an
   INTEGRITY event (Symptom 4), never rendered.
2. **Fans out per vendor** (the signed vendor list): resolves each vendor from
   ITS_Vendors (read-only), renders one **price-free** RFQ PDF per vendor (no money
   columns exist on an RFQ by design), files it to Box
   (`<job>/Purchase Orders/RFQs/`), writes the `RFQ_Log` (rfq, vendor) row, and stages
   an `RFQ_Pending_Review` row (Send Status `PENDING`; Workstream `po_materials_rfq`).
3. **Receipts** the RFQ back to the Worker (mark-filed) ONCE, LAST — a crash before the
   receipt simply re-serves the RFQ next cycle and every prior step re-runs
   find-or-skip (no duplicates).
4. **Mirrors** review-sheet SENT stamps back to the Worker (status-sync) and stamps the
   RFQ_Log row `sent`.

**Nothing here emails a vendor.** The actual send is the separate `rfq_send` half
(PR-D) with its own review/approval flow (F22) and its own go-live. An RFQ row a human
never approves simply sits `PENDING` forever — that is correct behavior, not a fault.

> **The both-rule (FM v11 / Op Stds §44).** A fault is Tier-2-eligible (the
> Successor-Operator may self-repair) only if it is **documented here AND
> low-capability-class**. Anything touching the **External Send Gate, secrets/auth,
> doctrine, or code** is FIXED high-class → escalate to Seth (the Developer-Operator)
> regardless of documentation. For `rfq_poll` the most common escalations are an
> **HMAC failure** (security) and **missing/rejected credentials** (secrets/auth).

## The one-shot flag file — how PERMANENT refusals are retried

Permanent refusals (HMAC failures, malformed due dates, empty/unknown vendor lists) are
recorded in `state/rfq_poll_flagged.json` as `{rfq_id: reason}` and **skipped every
subsequent cycle** (no Review-Queue spam every 120 s).

**Retry after fixing the cause = delete the RFQ's entry from that file** (or the whole
file) — the daemon re-attempts it on the next cycle. Deleting a flag entry is a
low-class Tier-2 repair.

## Symptom 1 — "Composed RFQs are not being pulled / nothing appears in RFQ_Log"

**Check:** ITS_Daemon_Health row `po_materials.rfq_poll` (stale/missing?), then
ITS_Config `po_materials.rfq_poll.polling_enabled` (Workstream `po_materials`).

- Gate `false` → the lane is dark **by design**. Activation is an operator decision:
  flip requires the Worker deployed with the RFQ routes, Keychain
  `ITS_PORTAL_RFQ_TOKEN` present, and the two sheets built
  (`build_rfq_log_sheet.py`, `build_rfq_pending_review_sheet.py`) with their ids
  flipped in `shared/sheet_ids.py`. Read the gate row's full Description first
  (HOUSE_REFLEXES §5).
- Gate `true` but no heartbeat → the plist may not be loaded:
  `scripts/launchd/install.sh status org.solutionsmith.its.rfq-poll`, then
  `install.sh load org.solutionsmith.its.rfq-poll`. Low-class.
- Heartbeat `ERROR` with `rfq_creds_missing` → **escalate (secrets/auth)**: the Worker
  base URL and/or the RFQ bearer / HMAC-secret Keychain entries are unset.

## Symptom 2 — "ITS_Errors shows `rfq_bearer_rejected` (CRITICAL)"

The Worker rejected the RFQ bearer (401). This will not self-heal — the token was
rotated on one side only. **Escalate to Seth (secrets/auth, FIXED high-class).**
Everything stays queued; no data is lost while it waits.

## Symptom 3 — "One vendor's copy is missing; the Review Queue says `vendor … not found in ITS_Vendors`"

The RFQ named a vendor key with no ITS_Vendors row (or a retired one). The OTHER
vendors' copies filed normally — only this vendor was fenced. Repair (low-class):

1. Fix/add the vendor row in ITS_Vendors (Vendor Key must match exactly, `VEN-######`).
2. The RFQ has already been receipted (it will not re-serve). Re-issue THIS vendor's
   copy by composing a new RFQ to that vendor in the portal, OR escalate if the
   original RFQ must be preserved verbatim.
3. If **ALL** vendors were fenced, the RFQ was NOT receipted — it re-serves once you
   fix the vendor rows and delete the `{rfq_id: "vendors_fenced"}` entry from
   `state/rfq_poll_flagged.json`.

## Symptom 4 — "ITS_Errors shows `rfq_hmac_failure` (CRITICAL) / a security-flagged Review-Queue row"

A queued RFQ failed signature verification — the row was altered after the Worker
signed it (or Worker↔Mac key/protocol skew after a deploy). The RFQ was **never
rendered and never filed**; the raw row stays in D1 for forensics.
**Escalate to Seth immediately (security, FIXED high-class).** Do not delete the flag
entry, do not purge the D1 row.

## Symptom 5 — "RFQ filed but the review row / ledger row is missing or duplicated"

- Missing review-row or ledger-row file attachment with WARN `rfq_row_file_attach_failed`
  → cosmetic; the Box link on the row is the SoR copy. Low-class: ignore or re-attach by
  hand (both the review row AND the flat RFQ_Log row carry the RFQ PDF + quote form
  inline — PO-lane parity, 2026-07-20).
- A transient Smartsheet/Box failure (`rfq_filing_transient`) leaves the RFQ queued —
  it self-heals next cycle; duplicates are prevented by find-or-skip (RFQ Number +
  Vendor Key on the ledger; the Notes `rfq_id`/`vendor_key` join on the review sheet).
- A genuinely duplicated row (should not happen) → escalate with the row links.

## Symptom 5b — per-job RFQ tracking sheet mirror failed (`rfq_perjob_sheet_failed`, WARN, permanent one-shot miss)

The po_poll Symptom-13 class, RFQ edition. The RFQ itself **filed normally** (Box PDF +
quote form, flat RFQ_Log row with inline attachments, review row, mark-filed receipt);
only the supplementary per-job mirror row (ITS — Purchase Orders / **Jobs** / `<job>` /
**"RFQs"** sheet, `shared/job_sheet.py`) is missing. Best-effort by design — **no
auto-retry; a failed mirror append is a PERMANENT one-shot miss** unless repaired by hand.
**Low-class Tier-2 repair:** find the (RFQ Number, Vendor Key) row in the flat RFQ_Log,
copy it into the job's "RFQs" sheet (a structure-only clone — the columns match by
construction). The flat RFQ_Log + Box remain the SoR; nothing send-path is affected.
Related lower-level WARNs (`shared.job_sheet` script name): `job_sheet_ready_probe_exhausted`,
`job_sheet_folder_race_duplicate` / `job_sheet_sheet_race_duplicate`, `sheet_capacity_*` —
see `docs/runbooks/po_poll.md` Symptom 13 for their handling; they are identical here.

## Symptom 6 — "A SENT RFQ still shows `filed` in RFQ_Log"

Pass ② mirrors SENT stamps only after a successful status-sync POST. Check ITS_Errors
for `rfq_status_sync_failed` (transient — self-heals next cycle) or
`rfq_status_sheet_placeholder` (the review sheet id was never flipped — run the
builder + flip, low-class). A review row missing its Notes join
(`rfq_id=…; vendor_key=…`) can never sync — escalate if someone edited Notes.

## Escalate-to-Seth boundary (summary)

| Event | Class |
|---|---|
| Gate flip to `true` (lane activation) | Operator decision — read the Description; the SEND half's go-live is FIXED high-class |
| `rfq_hmac_failure` | **Security — escalate** |
| `rfq_bearer_rejected` / `rfq_creds_missing` | **Secrets/auth — escalate** |
| Unknown-vendor fence | Tier-2 (fix vendor row; re-issue or unflag) |
| Flag-entry deletion after a fixed cause | Tier-2 |
| Anything requiring a code or Worker change | **Code — escalate** |
